articles.title: Why isn't Maxi in the rotation?
articles.original_title: Why isn't Maxi in the rotation?
articles.summary: Am I the only one who is frustrated and can't wrap their head around the fact that Maxi hardly sees the floor? After the Kings game, Luka mentioned how much he loves playing with Maxi and how they have great chemistry on the floor after all that time in Dallas. He's far from a liability on defense, and despite inconsistent shooting numbers (which usually improved the more he was on the court in Dallas), he's at least capable of setting good screens and rolling hard to the rim. What am I missing?

articles.summary: "SGA, he's a tough cover, and he does a great job of convincing the referees -- probably better than anybody in the league -- that he's getting hit," Knicks coach Mike Brown said after his team's 103-100 loss to the Thunder on Wednesday night.

articles.title: Judge rules Beasley owes $1M to former agency
articles.original_title: Judge rules Beasley owes $1M to former agency
articles.summary: A federal judge in New York issued a $1 million civil judgment against NBA free agent Malik Beasley on Thursday in a contract dispute with his former agency.

articles.title: Caruso, Hartenstein exit OKC win with injuries
articles.original_title: Caruso, Hartenstein exit OKC win with injuries
articles.summary: Alex Caruso (left hip contusion) and Isaiah Hartenstein (left calf tightness) were ruled out during the team's 103-100 victory over the Knicks on Wednesday night and will be reevaluated Thursday.

articles.title: Tottenham loses again and faces fight for Premier League survival
articles.original_title: Tottenham loses again and faces fight for Premier League survival
articles.summary: Tottenham's Premier League survival hopes took another blow on Thursday with a 3-1 loss to Crystal Palace.  Spurs — one of the richest teams in Europe and a founding member of the Premier League — is just one point above the relegation zone and without a domestic win in 2026.  The latest defeat was the fifth in a row and the third under new coach Igor Tudor, who was tasked with the responsibility of turning the season around.

articles.title: Gibb scores 19 points and BYU beats Utah for 3rd time season, 70-52, to advance to Big 12 quarters
articles.original_title: Gibb scores 19 points and BYU beats Utah for 3rd time season, 70-52, to advance to Big 12 quarters
articles.summary: Delaney Gibb had 19 points, eight assists and four steals to lead BYU to a 70-52 win over rival Utah in the second round of the Big 12 Tournament on Thursday.  The ninth-seeded Cougars (22-10) won their fifth straight game and beat the eighth-seeded Utes (19-12) for the third time this season to advance to play top seed and No. 10-ranked TCU in a Friday quarterfinal.  Gibb made four 3-pointers as did Brinley Cannon, who scored 12 points.
